title: UC Davis - Luminex/Multiplex
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9g50d","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"22f5v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6oip3","text":"This core will provide services for quantification and comparison in animal models for hormone measurements; kidney function analytes; lipid metabolism analytes; liver function analytes; miscellaneous protein analytes; and immune function including cytokines/chemokines. The Luminex assay enables the quantification of all or any combination of analytes for which there are assay beads in tissue/cell lysate, tissue culture supernatant samples and plasma/serum. For example, cytokines and chemokines are peptides, which act as regulators in normal and pathological conditions, effecting interactions between cells as well as regulating processes occurring in the extracellular environment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6a7hc","text":"   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b8crq","text":"IMPORTANT: All plasma, serum, tissue supernatants must be centrifuged for 10 minutes at 12,000 rpms to clarify the biological fluid. Avoid multiple freeze thaws \u003e2. All reagents must be prepared prior to step one and be at room temperature, beads must be sonicated and vortexed, standards and QC controls reconstituted and after 10 minutes and transferred to a polypropylene tube. Always place filter plate on the plate stand except when blotting post wash. Thorough blotting post wash and pre-incubation is critical to avoid leakage due to capillary action. During the incubation step, the filter plate must be covered with a plate sealer and wrapped in foil due to light sensitivity of the beads. Never invert the plate. Vacuum suction \u003c100mmHg.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

For pre-mixed beads, sonicate each bottle for 30 seconds. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a60sd","text":"c. Vortex each antibody-bead vial for one minute.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8capo","text":"d. For pre-mixed beads, vortex bottle for one minute.     ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2sfdb","text":"e. For individual beads, add 60 ul from each individual antibody-bead vial   to the Mixing Bottle and bring final volume to 3.0 ml with bead diluent.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3t0ln","text":"f. Vortex the mixed beads/bead diluent well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3a9od","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"clo99","text":"2. Before use, reconstitute Quality Control 1 with 250 µl of deionized water.    ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9vnv6","text":"b. Before use, reconstitute Quality Control 2 with 250 ul of deionized water. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ba3su","text":"c. Invert Quality Control 1 and Quality Control 2 vials several times. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"63b06","text":"d. Vortex gently Quality Control 1 and Quality Control 2 vials.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cll1q","text":"e. Allow both vials to sit for 10 minutes at room temperature. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fktdd","text":"f. Transfer Quality Control 1 and Quality Control 2 contents to appropriately labeled polypropylene microfuge tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhqei","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3pre1","text":"3. Bring the 10X Wash Buffer to room temperature. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"921k1","text":"b. Dilute 30 ml of 10X Wash Buffer with 270 ml of deionized water. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7fk8o","text":"c. Wash Buffer should be room temperature before use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1mhk9","text":"d. Wash Buffer can be stored at 2-8 degrees C for up to one month.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9v3g6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8cot9","text":"4. Reconstitute the Human Cytokine Standard with 250 ul of deionized water. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9omah","text":"b. Reconstituted standard concentration will be 10,000 pg/ml. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c7gqf","text":"c. Invert the vial several times to mix. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3gc44","text":"d. Vortex the vial for 10 seconds. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47j66","text":"e. Allow the vial to sit for 5-10 minutes at room temperature. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7tjgu","text":"f. Transfer contents to an appropriately labeled polypropylene microfuge tube. steps: Pre-wet the filter plate with 200 ul assay buffer, agitate, incubate for 10 minutes RT and vacuum.
Add 25 ul of standards, controls, assay buffer, samples, matrix serum and beads to appropriate wells on the filter plate.
Place filter plate on plate stand, seal, wrap in foil and incubate overnight while agitating on a Titer Plate Shaker at 4 degrees C (16-18 hours).
Remove fluid from the filter plate by vacuum and wash. Bolt well.
Add 25 ul of detection antibody to each well, place filter plate on plate stand, seal, wrap in foil, agitate on a Titer Plate Shaker for 1 hour at RT.

Without removing detection antibody, add 25 ul of Streptavidin- Phycoerythrin to each well. Place filter plate on plate stand, seal, wrap in foil, agitate on the Titer Plate Shaker. Incubate for 30 mins RT.
Remove the fluid by vacuum and wash the plate. Bolt well.
Add 150 uls of Sheath Fluid to each well, place on plate stand, seal, wrap in foil, agitate on the Titer Plate Shaker at RT for 5 minutes.
Set up protocol on the Bio-Plex 200: add analytes and regions, standards, quality controls and sample dilutions to Bio-Plex 6.0 Manager software.
Save and analyze the median fluorescent intensity data using a 5-parameter logistic or spline curve fitting method for calculating cytokine/chemokines concentrations in samples in pg/ml.
creator: Lili Liang
